mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust economic expansion, leading to higher aggregate demand, stronger corporate earnings expectations, and positive equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between PAYEMS monthly change and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a sharp economic contraction and rising credit defaults, leading to lower expected earnings and negative equity returns as investors de-risk.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates while the policy rate (FEDFUNDS) remains near zero, it signals the central bank is behind the curve, increasing the risk of a future hard landing and negatively impacting equity valuations via higher discount rates.
failure_condition: A two-factor model does not outperform a single-factor CPIAUCSL model in walk-forward information ratio over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) confirms a cyclical upswing, boosting investor confidence and driving positive equity returns. This effect diminishes or reverses late in the cycle as capacity constraints emerge.
failure_condition: The signal's predictive power (rank IC) is not significantly higher in post-recession periods (UNRATE > 6%) compared to late-cycle periods (UNRATE < 4.5%).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp positive surprise in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates unexpectedly strong consumer demand, which translates quickly into higher revenue expectations for consumer-facing sectors and lifts the broader market in the short term.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically different from the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An extremely low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for future corporate earnings and encourages risk-taking by lowering the return on safe assets, leading to higher equity valuations over the medium term.
regime_hint: monetary easing or Zero Interest Rate Policy (ZIRP)
failure_condition: The average 60-day forward S&P 500 return when the trigger is active is not statistically higher than when it is inactive.

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer confidence and investment, acting as a leading indicator for a broader economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate profits and equity prices.
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger, the S&P 500 experiences positive 60-day returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) is positive but decelerating, while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating, this 'stagflationary' environment erodes corporate margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ % change > 0 AND GDPC1 QoQ % change is decelerating AND CPIAUCSL YoY % change is accelerating
failure_condition: A regression model on forward returns shows a non-negative coefficient for the interaction term between GDPC1 momentum and CPIAUCSL momentum.
